speaker: DUKE
text: Sir Thurio, give us leave, I pray, awhile;

speaker: DUKE
text: We have some secrets to confer about.

speaker: DUKE
text: Now tell me, Proteus, what’s your will with me?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: My gracious lord, that which I would discover

speaker: PROTEUS
text: The law of friendship bids me to conceal,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But when I call to mind your gracious favors

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Done to me, undeserving as I am,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: My duty pricks me on to utter that

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Which else no worldly good should draw from me.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Know, worthy prince, Sir Valentine my friend

speaker: PROTEUS
text: This night intends to steal away your daughter;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Myself am one made privy to the plot.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I know you have determined to bestow her

speaker: PROTEUS
text: On Thurio, whom your gentle daughter hates,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And should she thus be stol’n away from you,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: It would be much vexation to your age.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Thus, for my duty’s sake, I rather chose

speaker: PROTEUS
text: To cross my friend in his intended drift

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Than, by concealing it, heap on your head

speaker: PROTEUS
text: A pack of sorrows which would press you down,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Being unprevented, to your timeless grave.

speaker: DUKE
text: Proteus, I thank thee for thine honest care,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which to requite command me while I live.

speaker: DUKE
text: This love of theirs myself have often seen,

speaker: DUKE
text: Haply when they have judged me fast asleep,

speaker: DUKE
text: And oftentimes have purposed to forbid

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir Valentine her company and my court.

speaker: DUKE
text: But fearing lest my jealous aim might err

speaker: DUKE
text: And so, unworthily, disgrace the man—

speaker: DUKE
text: A rashness that I ever yet have shunned—

speaker: DUKE
text: I gave him gentle looks, thereby to find

speaker: DUKE
text: That which thyself hast now disclosed to me.

speaker: DUKE
text: And that thou mayst perceive my fear of this,

speaker: DUKE
text: Knowing that tender youth is soon suggested,

speaker: DUKE
text: I nightly lodge her in an upper tower,

speaker: DUKE
text: The key whereof myself have ever kept,

speaker: DUKE
text: And thence she cannot be conveyed away.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Know, noble lord, they have devised a mean

speaker: PROTEUS
text: How he her chamber-window will ascend

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And with a corded ladder fetch her down;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: For which the youthful lover now is gone,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And this way comes he with it presently,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Where, if it please you, you may intercept him.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But, good my lord, do it so cunningly

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That my discovery be not aimèd at;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: For love of you, not hate unto my friend,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Hath made me publisher of this pretense.

speaker: DUKE
text: Upon mine honor, he shall never know

speaker: DUKE
text: That I had any light from thee of this.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Adieu, my lord. Sir Valentine is coming.

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir Valentine, whither away so fast?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Please it your Grace, there is a messenger

speaker: VALENTINE
text: That stays to bear my letters to my friends,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And I am going to deliver them.

speaker: DUKE
text: Be they of much import?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: The tenor of them doth but signify

speaker: VALENTINE
text: My health and happy being at your court.

speaker: DUKE
text: Nay then, no matter. Stay with me awhile;

speaker: DUKE
text: I am to break with thee of some affairs

speaker: DUKE
text: That touch me near, wherein thou must be secret.

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Tis not unknown to thee that I have sought

speaker: DUKE
text: To match my friend Sir Thurio to my daughter.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I know it well, my lord, and sure the match

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Were rich and honorable. Besides, the gentleman

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is full of virtue, bounty, worth, and qualities

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Beseeming such a wife as your fair daughter.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Cannot your Grace win her to fancy him?

speaker: DUKE
text: No. Trust me, she is peevish, sullen, froward,

speaker: DUKE
text: Proud, disobedient, stubborn, lacking duty,

speaker: DUKE
text: Neither regarding that she is my child

speaker: DUKE
text: Nor fearing me as if I were her father;

speaker: DUKE
text: And may I say to thee, this pride of hers,

speaker: DUKE
text: Upon advice, hath drawn my love from her,

speaker: DUKE
text: And where I thought the remnant of mine age

speaker: DUKE
text: Should have been cherished by her childlike duty,

speaker: DUKE
text: I now am full resolved to take a wife

speaker: DUKE
text: And turn her out to who will take her in.

speaker: DUKE
text: Then let her beauty be her wedding dower,

speaker: DUKE
text: For me and my possessions she esteems not.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: What would your Grace have me to do in this?

speaker: DUKE
text: There is a lady in Verona here

speaker: DUKE
text: Whom I affect; but she is nice, and coy,

speaker: DUKE
text: And nought esteems my agèd eloquence.

speaker: DUKE
text: Now therefore would I have thee to my tutor—

speaker: DUKE
text: For long agone I have forgot to court;

speaker: DUKE
text: Besides, the fashion of the time is changed—

speaker: DUKE
text: How and which way I may bestow myself

speaker: DUKE
text: To be regarded in her sun-bright eye.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Win her with gifts if she respect not words;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Dumb jewels often in their silent kind

speaker: VALENTINE
text: More than quick words do move a woman’s mind.

speaker: DUKE
text: But she did scorn a present that I sent her.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: A woman sometime scorns what best contents her.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Send her another; never give her o’er,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: For scorn at first makes after-love the more.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If she do frown, ’tis not in hate of you,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But rather to beget more love in you.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If she do chide, ’tis not to have you gone,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Forwhy the fools are mad if left alone.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Take no repulse, whatever she doth say;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: For “get you gone” she doth not mean “away.”

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Flatter and praise, commend, extol their graces;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Though ne’er so black, say they have angels’ faces.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: That man that hath a tongue, I say, is no man

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If with his tongue he cannot win a woman.

speaker: DUKE
text: But she I mean is promised by her friends

speaker: DUKE
text: Unto a youthful gentleman of worth

speaker: DUKE
text: And kept severely from resort of men,

speaker: DUKE
text: That no man hath access by day to her.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Why, then, I would resort to her by night.

speaker: DUKE
text: Ay, but the doors be locked and keys kept safe,

speaker: DUKE
text: That no man hath recourse to her by night.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: What lets but one may enter at her window?

speaker: DUKE
text: Her chamber is aloft, far from the ground,

speaker: DUKE
text: And built so shelving that one cannot climb it

speaker: DUKE
text: Without apparent hazard of his life.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Why, then a ladder quaintly made of cords

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To cast up, with a pair of anchoring hooks,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Would serve to scale another Hero’s tower,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: So bold Leander would adventure it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Now, as thou art a gentleman of blood,

speaker: DUKE
text: Advise me where I may have such a ladder.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: When would you use it? Pray sir, tell me that.

speaker: DUKE
text: This very night; for love is like a child

speaker: DUKE
text: That longs for everything that he can come by.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: By seven o’clock I’ll get you such a ladder.

speaker: DUKE
text: But hark thee: I will go to her alone;

speaker: DUKE
text: How shall I best convey the ladder thither?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: It will be light, my lord, that you may bear it

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Under a cloak that is of any length.

speaker: DUKE
text: A cloak as long as thine will serve the turn?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Then let me see thy cloak;

speaker: DUKE
text: I’ll get me one of such another length.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Why, any cloak will serve the turn, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: How shall I fashion me to wear a cloak?

speaker: DUKE
text: I pray thee, let me feel thy cloak upon me.

speaker: DUKE
text:
What letter is this same? What’s here?
                 
                (
                Reads
                .
                )
               
                To
                 
                Sylvia
                .

speaker: DUKE
text: And here an engine fit for my proceeding.

speaker: DUKE
text: I’ll be so bold to break the seal for once.

speaker: DUKE
text: My thoughts do harbor with my Sylvia nightly,

speaker: DUKE
text: And slaves they are to me that send them flying.

speaker: DUKE
text: O, could their master come and go as lightly,

speaker: DUKE
text: Himself would lodge where, senseless, they are lying.

speaker: DUKE
text: My herald thoughts in thy pure bosom rest them,

speaker: DUKE
text: While I, their king, that thither them importune,

speaker: DUKE
text: Do curse the grace that with such grace hath blest them,

speaker: DUKE
text: Because myself do want my servants’ fortune.

speaker: DUKE
text: I curse myself, for they are sent by me,

speaker: DUKE
text: That they should harbor where their lord should be.

speaker: DUKE
text: What’s here?

speaker: DUKE
text: Sylvia, this night I will enfranchise thee.

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Tis so. And here’s the ladder for the purpose.

speaker: DUKE
text: Why, Phaëton—for thou art Merops’ son—

speaker: DUKE
text: Wilt thou aspire to guide the heavenly car

speaker: DUKE
text: And with thy daring folly burn the world?

speaker: DUKE
text: Wilt thou reach stars because they shine on thee?

speaker: DUKE
text: Go, base intruder, overweening slave,

speaker: DUKE
text: Bestow thy fawning smiles on equal mates

speaker: DUKE
text: And think my patience, more than thy desert,

speaker: DUKE
text: Is privilege for thy departure hence.

speaker: DUKE
text: Thank me for this more than for all the favors

speaker: DUKE
text: Which all too much I have bestowed on thee.

speaker: DUKE
text: But if thou linger in my territories

speaker: DUKE
text: Longer than swiftest expedition

speaker: DUKE
text: Will give thee time to leave our royal court,

speaker: DUKE
text: By heaven, my wrath shall far exceed the love

speaker: DUKE
text: I ever bore my daughter or thyself.

speaker: DUKE
text: Begone. I will not hear thy vain excuse,

speaker: DUKE
text: But, as thou lov’st thy life, make speed from hence.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And why not death, rather than living torment?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To die is to be banished from myself,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And Sylvia is myself; banished from her

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is self from self—a deadly banishment.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: What light is light if Sylvia be not seen?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: What joy is joy if Sylvia be not by—

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Unless it be to think that she is by

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And feed upon the shadow of perfection?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Except I be by Sylvia in the night,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: There is no music in the nightingale.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Unless I look on Sylvia in the day,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: There is no day for me to look upon.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: She is my essence, and I leave to be

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If I be not by her fair influence

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Fostered, illumined, cherished, kept alive.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I fly not death, to fly his deadly doom;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Tarry I here, I but attend on death,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But fly I hence, I fly away from life.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Run, boy, run, run, and seek him out.

speaker: LANCE
text: So-ho, so-ho!

speaker: PROTEUS
text: What seest thou?

speaker: LANCE
text: Him we go to find. There’s not a hair on ’s head

speaker: LANCE
text: but ’tis a Valentine.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Valentine?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: No.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Who then? His spirit?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Neither.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: What then?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Nothing.

speaker: LANCE
text: Can nothing speak? Master, shall I strike?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Who wouldst thou strike?

speaker: LANCE
text: Nothing.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Villain, forbear.

speaker: LANCE
text: Why, sir, I’ll strike nothing. I pray you—

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Sirrah, I say forbear.—Friend Valentine, a word.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: My ears are stopped and cannot hear good news,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: So much of bad already hath possessed them.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Then in dumb silence will I bury mine,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: For they are harsh, untunable, and bad.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is Sylvia dead?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: No, Valentine.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: No Valentine indeed for sacred Sylvia.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Hath she forsworn me?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: No, Valentine.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: No Valentine if Sylvia have forsworn me.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: What is your news?

speaker: LANCE
text: Sir, there is a proclamation that you are

speaker: LANCE
text: vanished.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That thou art banishèd—O, that’s the news—

speaker: PROTEUS
text: From hence, from Sylvia, and from me thy friend.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: O, I have fed upon this woe already,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And now excess of it will make me surfeit.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Doth Sylvia know that I am banishèd?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Ay, ay, and she hath offered to the doom—

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Which unreversed stands in effectual force—

speaker: PROTEUS
text: A sea of melting pearl, which some call tears;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Those at her father’s churlish feet she tendered,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: With them, upon her knees, her humble self,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Wringing her hands, whose whiteness so became them

speaker: PROTEUS
text: As if but now they waxèd pale for woe.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But neither bended knees, pure hands held up,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Sad sighs, deep groans, nor silver-shedding tears

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Could penetrate her uncompassionate sire;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But Valentine, if he be ta’en, must die.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Besides, her intercession chafed him so,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: When she for thy repeal was suppliant,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That to close prison he commanded her

speaker: PROTEUS
text: With many bitter threats of biding there.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: No more, unless the next word that thou speak’st

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Have some malignant power upon my life.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If so, I pray thee breathe it in mine ear

speaker: VALENTINE
text: As ending anthem of my endless dolor.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Cease to lament for that thou canst not help,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And study help for that which thou lament’st.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Time is the nurse and breeder of all good.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Here, if thou stay, thou canst not see thy love;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Besides, thy staying will abridge thy life.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Hope is a lover’s staff; walk hence with that

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And manage it against despairing thoughts.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Thy letters may be here, though thou art hence,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Which, being writ to me, shall be delivered

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Even in the milk-white bosom of thy love.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: The time now serves not to expostulate.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Come, I’ll convey thee through the city gate

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And, ere I part with thee, confer at large

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Of all that may concern thy love affairs.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: As thou lov’st Sylvia, though not for thyself,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Regard thy danger, and along with me.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I pray thee, Lance, an if thou seest my boy,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Bid him make haste and meet me at the North Gate.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Go, sirrah, find him out.—Come, Valentine.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: O, my dear Sylvia! Hapless Valentine!

speaker: LANCE
text: I am but a fool, look you, and yet I have the wit

speaker: LANCE
text: to think my master is a kind of a knave, but that’s all

speaker: LANCE
text: one if he be but one knave. He lives not now that

speaker: LANCE
text: knows me to be in love, yet I am in love, but a team

speaker: LANCE
text: of horse shall not pluck that from me, nor who ’tis I

speaker: LANCE
text: love; and yet ’tis a woman, but what woman I will

speaker: LANCE
text: not tell myself; and yet ’tis a milk-maid; yet ’tis not a

speaker: LANCE
text: maid, for she hath had gossips; yet ’tis a maid, for

speaker: LANCE
text: she is her master’s maid and serves for wages. She

speaker: LANCE
text: hath more qualities than a water spaniel, which is

speaker: LANCE
text: much in a bare Christian.

speaker: LANCE
text: Here is the catalog of her condition.

speaker: LANCE
text:
(
                Reads
                .
                )
               Imprimis, She can fetch and carry. Why, a

speaker: LANCE
text: horse can do no more; nay, a horse cannot fetch but

speaker: LANCE
text: only carry; therefore is she better than a jade.

speaker: LANCE
text:
(
                Reads
                .
                )
               
                Item
                ,
                 
                She
                 
                can
                 
                milk
                .
               Look you, a sweet

speaker: LANCE
text: virtue in a maid with clean hands.

speaker: SPEED
text: How now, Signior Lance? What news with your

speaker: SPEED
text: Mastership?

speaker: LANCE
text: With my master’s ship? Why, it is at sea.

speaker: SPEED
text: Well, your old vice still: mistake the word. What

speaker: SPEED
text: news, then, in your paper?

speaker: LANCE
text: The black’st news that ever thou heard’st.

speaker: SPEED
text: Why, man? How black?

speaker: LANCE
text: Why, as black as ink.

speaker: SPEED
text: Let me read them.

speaker: LANCE
text: Fie on thee, jolt-head, thou canst not read.

speaker: SPEED
text: Thou liest. I can.

speaker: LANCE
text: I will try thee. Tell me this, who begot thee?

speaker: SPEED
text: Marry, the son of my grandfather.

speaker: LANCE
text: O, illiterate loiterer, it was the son of thy grandmother.

speaker: LANCE
text: This proves that thou canst not read.

speaker: SPEED
text: Come, fool, come. Try me in thy paper.

speaker: LANCE
text: There, and Saint Nicholas

speaker: LANCE
text: be thy speed.

speaker: SPEED
text:
reads
            
            
              
              Imprimis
              ,
               
              She
               
              can
               
              milk
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: Ay, that she can.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              brews
               
              good
               
              ale
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: And thereof comes the proverb: “Blessing of

speaker: LANCE
text: your heart, you brew good ale.”

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              can
               
              sew
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: That’s as much as to say “Can she so?”

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              can
               
              knit
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: What need a man care for a stock with a wench,

speaker: LANCE
text: when she can knit him a stock?

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              can
               
              wash
               
              and
               
              scour
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: A special virtue, for then she need not be

speaker: LANCE
text: washed and scoured.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              can
               
              spin
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: Then may I set the world on wheels, when she

speaker: LANCE
text: can spin for her living.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              hath
               
              many
               
              nameless
               
              virtues
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: That’s as much as to say “bastard virtues,” that

speaker: LANCE
text: indeed know not their fathers and therefore have no

speaker: LANCE
text: names.

speaker: SPEED
text: Here follow her vices.

speaker: LANCE
text: Close at the heels of her virtues.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              is
               
              not
               
              to
               
              be
               
              kissed
               
              fasting
               
              in
               
              respect
               
              of
              
              her
               
              breath
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: Well, that fault may be mended with a breakfast.

speaker: LANCE
text: Read on.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              hath
               
              a
               
              sweet
               
              mouth
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: That makes amends for her sour breath.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              doth
               
              talk
               
              in
               
              her
               
              sleep
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: It’s no matter for that, so she sleep not in her

speaker: LANCE
text: talk.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              is
               
              slow
               
              in
               
              words
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: O villain, that set this down among her vices! To

speaker: LANCE
text: be slow in words is a woman’s only virtue. I pray

speaker: LANCE
text: thee, out with ’t, and place it for her chief virtue.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              is
               
              proud
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: Out with that too; it was Eve’s legacy and

speaker: LANCE
text: cannot be ta’en from her.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              hath
               
              no
               
              teeth
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: I care not for that neither, because I love crusts.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              is
               
              curst
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: Well, the best is, she hath no teeth to bite.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              will
               
              often
               
              praise
               
              her
               
              liquor
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: If her liquor be good, she shall; if she will not, I

speaker: LANCE
text: will, for good things should be praised.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,
               
              She
               
              is
               
              too
               
              liberal
              .

speaker: LANCE
text: Of her tongue she cannot, for that’s writ down

speaker: LANCE
text: she is slow of; of her purse she shall not, for that I’ll

speaker: LANCE
text: keep shut; now, of another thing she may, and that

speaker: LANCE
text: cannot I help. Well, proceed.

speaker: SPEED
text:

              ,
               
              She
               
              hath
               
              more
               
              hair
               
              than
               
              wit
              ,

speaker: LANCE
text: Stop there. I’ll have her. She was mine and not

speaker: LANCE
text: mine twice or thrice in that last article. Rehearse

speaker: LANCE
text: that once more.

speaker: SPEED
text:
Item
              ,

              .

speaker: LANCE
text:
“
                More
                 
                hair
                 
                than
                 
                wit
                ”
              ? It may be; I’ll prove it:

speaker: LANCE
text: the cover of the salt hides the salt, and therefore it is

speaker: LANCE
text: more than the salt; the hair that covers the wit is

speaker: LANCE
text: more than the wit, for the greater hides the less.

speaker: LANCE
text: What’s next?

text:

speaker: LANCE
text: That’s monstrous! O, that that were out!

speaker: SPEED
text:
And

speaker: LANCE
text: Why, that word makes the faults gracious. Well,

speaker: LANCE
text: I’ll have her, and if it be a match, as nothing is

speaker: LANCE
text: impossible—

speaker: SPEED
text: What then?

speaker: LANCE
text: Why, then will I tell thee that thy master stays

speaker: LANCE
text: for thee at the North Gate.

speaker: SPEED
text: For me?

speaker: LANCE
text: For thee? Ay, who art thou? He hath stayed for a

speaker: LANCE
text: better man than thee.

speaker: SPEED
text: And must I go to him?

speaker: LANCE
text: Thou must run to him, for thou hast stayed so

speaker: LANCE
text: long that going will scarce serve the turn.

speaker: SPEED
text: Why didst not tell me

speaker: SPEED
text: sooner? Pox of your love letters!

speaker: LANCE
text: Now will he be swinged for reading my letter;

speaker: LANCE
text: an unmannerly slave, that will thrust himself into

speaker: LANCE
text: secrets. I’ll after, to rejoice in the boy’s correction.
